should_trigger: We have six months of agent execution logs with task ids, step names, timestamps, retries, and failures. Help redesign the orchestrator by discovering the real workflow and bottlenecks.
Compare our intended approval workflow with the actual traces from production and identify where cases bypass required validation.
Use historical run events to find why some research jobs get stuck, which variants are most expensive, and where to add predictive intervention.
Given span logs for tool calls and handoffs, infer the common process variants and recommend routing changes for the auto-orchestrator.
should_not_trigger: Design a brand new orchestration workflow from requirements only; we do not have logs or historical executions yet.
Write a generic summary of what process mining is for a classroom presentation.
Prove that this state machine can never deadlock using formal verification.
edge_cases: We have logs, but activity names are inconsistent free text and many cases are missing end timestamps. Decide whether process mining is usable yet and what instrumentation must be fixed first.
The discovered dominant path is faster but skips a policy-mandated review step. Decide how to interpret the finding for orchestrator redesign.

compliance_expectations.should_trigger: Uses the process-mining workflow; defines case/activity/timestamp contract, checks event-log quality, discovers real process variants, checks conformance against the intended model, mines performance/resource/decision perspectives, converts findings into orchestrator changes, and defines a rerun plan.
compliance_expectations.should_not_trigger: Does not activate the skill; answers as requirements design, classroom summary, formal verification, or another narrower task when historical case-level execution data is absent or irrelevant.
compliance_expectations.edge_cases: For weak logs, stops interpretation and recommends instrumentation repairs. For faster nonconforming paths, classifies the deviation before recommending policy, implementation, or exception-handling changes.
